calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Oct-02-2008 Text Ruling

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Sep-05-2008 Continued To Oct-02-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: 10:12am
Judge Pro Tem Elliott Myles, nothing on record, clerk-beth wilber, Dept. 525:
Cause called for Small Claims appeal.
Appellant, Victoria Singleton, appears in Pro Per; no appearance by Respondent, Jesse M. Bender.
Ms. Singleton, previously sworn, informs the Court that there has been a settlement, but that she has not been paid yet. The matter is continued to Oct-02-08, 9:00am. No evidence is marked or retained. (Ms. Singleton & Ms. Bender previously signed a stip to allow the matter to be heard by Judge Pro Tem Elliott Myles and will not need to sign a new one.)

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Continued To Sep-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem Elliott Myles, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record, Dept. 525:
10:30AM
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Respondent, Jesse M. Bender, appears in Pro Per. Parties previously sworn testify in the narrative; Appellant, Victoria Singleton, appears in Pro Per. Plaintiff, Children's Council of San Francisco is not present. Parties previously sworn testify in the narrative. Judge Myles determines that he will treat the appeal of Ms. Singleton as separate from plaintiff Children's Council. (It is determined by the Court that Children's Council had previously paid their judgment.) Parties, previously sworn, testify in the narrative.
11:00AM
The matter is continued, for good cause, to 9/4/08, 9:00AM, Dept. 525. The continuance is to allow Ms. Bender to find the agreement she signed with CC, that specifies the subsidy terms. The Court advises parties to try and reach a settlement. If a settlement is reached Ms. Singleton will file a dismissal of the action.

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Jun-16-2008 Judgment Entered
